vocab.meaning: thu nhập khả dụng (tiền dư sau khi trừ thuế và chi phí thiết yếu)
vocab.example: I only spend a small fraction of my disposable income on footwear, as I'm quite mindful of my savings.

vocab.meaning: hàng đặt may riêng, đóng riêng theo yêu cầu
vocab.example: I can’t quite afford bespoke shoes yet, but I dream of owning a pair tailored specifically to my feet.

vocab.meaning: mua sắm để xả stress, giải tỏa tâm trạng
vocab.example: On the rare occasion I engage in retail therapy, I might spend a bit more on a high-end pair of boots.

vocab.meaning: độ bền quan trọng hơn vẻ ngoài
vocab.example: I prioritize durability over aesthetics, so I'm willing to pay a premium for shoes that won't fall apart in six months.

vocab.meaning: người săn hàng giá rẻ/giảm giá
vocab.example: As a dedicated bargain hunter, I rarely pay full price for shoes, preferring to wait for the end-of-season clearance.

vocab.meaning: một lần vung tay quá trán, mua đồ xa xỉ
vocab.example: Buying those designer heels was a bit of a splurge, but they were perfect for my brother's wedding.

vocab.meaning: các thương hiệu phân khúc tầm trung
vocab.example: I typically stick to mid-range brands that offer a decent balance between comfort and cost.

vocab.meaning: việc mua sắm ngẫu hứng (không kế hoạch trước)
vocab.example: I try to avoid impulse buys when I'm at the mall, as I often end up with shoes I rarely wear.

vocab.meaning: món đồ đáng để đầu tư (đắt nhưng dùng lâu dài)
vocab.example: I view a solid pair of leather brogues as an investment piece that will stay in my wardrobe for a decade.
I’ve found that the $100 price point usually guarantees a level of comfort that cheaper alternatives lack.
